entities.name: HARDSETTING ALFISOL
entities.description: A type of soil characterized by hardsetting properties in semi-arid regions

entities.name: SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: The climatic region where the study was conducted

entities.name: HYDERABAD, INDIA
entities.description: The specific location of the ICRISAT experimental site

entities.name: INFILTRATION
entities.description: The process by which water on the ground surface enters the soil

entities.name: EROSION
entities.description: The process of wearing away soil by water or wind

entities.name: RANDOMISED BLOCK DESIGN
entities.description: The experimental design used to apply treatments to plots

entities.name: TILLAGE
entities.description: The agricultural practice of preparing soil by mechanical agitation

entities.name: MULCH
entities.description: The practice of covering soil with organic or inorganic material to protect it

entities.name: FARMYARD MANURE
entities.description: An organic mulch and fertilizer treatment

entities.name: RICE STRAW
entities.description: An organic mulch treatment derived from rice crop residues

entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: Pennisetum glaucum, the primary crop sown in the factorial sub-set

entities.name: PERENNIAL PIGEONPEA
entities.description: Cajanus cajan, a perennial species used in ley treatments

entities.name: VERANO STYLO
entities.description: Stylosanthes hamata, a perennial species used in ley treatments

entities.name: BUFFEL GRASS
entities.description: Cenchrus ciliaris, a perennial species used in ley treatments

entities.name: RUNOFF
entities.description: The draining away of water from the surface of an area of land

entities.name: SOIL LOSS
entities.description: The amount of soil removed from the site by erosion

entities.name: STRAW YIELD
entities.description: The amount of straw produced by the crop

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced by the crop

entities.name: SUSPENDED LOAD
entities.description: Soil particles carried in suspension within runoff water

entities.name: BED LOAD
entities.description: Soil particles transported along the bottom of the runoff flow

entities.name: RAINDROP DETACHMENT
entities.description: The process where raindrops break apart soil particles, leading to erosion

entities.name: SUSTAINABILITY OF FARMING SYSTEMS
entities.description: The long-term viability and health of agricultural practices

relationships.description: Tillage affects the volume of runoff
Mulch reduces the volume of runoff
Rice straw mulch significantly reduces runoff compared to FYM and no mulch
FYM significantly increases millet grain yield
Tillage to 10 and 20 cm significantly reduces grain yield
Tillage to 20 cm reduces millet straw yield
Tillage significantly increases suspended load concentration in early runoff events
Rice straw mulch significantly reduces bed load concentration
Rice straw mulch reduces suspended load in later runoff events
Raindrop detachment is identified as the main erosion process
Hardsetting Alfisols are the target soil for reducing erosion
Pearl millet is the crop measured for grain yield
Pearl millet is the crop measured for straw yield
Perennial pigeonpea treatments tend to have higher runoff than millet
Verano stylo treatments tend to have higher runoff than millet
Buffel grass treatments tend to have higher runoff than millet
Mulch-based systems contribute to the sustainability of farming systems
Tillage-based systems have a limited role in sustainability
